title: Fix admin dashboard inquiries not loading (showing 'refresh the page' message)

requirements.text: Diagnose and fix the data fetching issue in AdminDashboardPage.tsx that causes the inquiries table to display a 'refresh the page' message instead of rendering submitted inquiries. Investigate the useGetAllInquiries React Query hook in useQueries.ts and the corresponding backend getInquiries function in backend/main.mo. Ensure the query correctly calls the backend actor, handles the response (including Motoko Result/variant unwrapping), and surfaces real inquiry data in the table. Eliminate any condition or error path that triggers the 'refresh the page' fallback message when inquiries are present or the fetch is in progress.

quotes: The inquiries are not showing its is showing refresh the page

acceptanceCriteria: After entering the correct admin password ('luckygoyal0001'), submitted inquiries are displayed in the table without any 'refresh the page' message.
The React Query hook in useQueries.ts correctly invokes the backend actor's getInquiries function and returns the inquiry array.
The backend getInquiries function in main.mo returns all stored inquiries without errors or access-control rejections for any caller.
A loading skeleton is shown while the query is in flight.
An empty-state message is shown only when zero inquiries have been submitted (not as a result of a fetch error).
Any error state shows a descriptive error message rather than the generic 'refresh the page' fallback.

constraints: Do not change the admin password; it must remain 'luckygoyal0001'.
Do not re-introduce Internet Identity or any third-party authentication.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Adding new inquiry fields or changing the inquiry data model.
Changing the visual design or layout of the Admin Dashboard beyond fixing the data display bug.
Adding pagination or sorting to the inquiries table.
